etymology.description: 拉手 (lāshǒu, "hold hands") consists of 拉(拉) (lā, "pull") + 手(手) (shǒu, "hand").
etymology.interpretation: 拉(拉) (lā, "pull") involves motion toward oneself, evoking closeness or connection when applied to 手(手) (shǒu, "hand"). Therefore, 拉手 (lāshǒu) literally describes the action of pulling hands together, thus intuitively depicting the act of holding hands.

examples.chinese: 在公园里，我们常常拉手(在公園裏，我們常常拉手)。
examples.pinyin: Zài gōngyuán lǐ, wǒmen chángcháng lāshǒu.
examples.english: In the park, we often hold hands.

examples.chinese: 小孩拉着手，一起过马路(小孩拉著手，一起過馬路)。
examples.pinyin: Xiǎohái lāzhe shǒu, yīqǐ guò mǎlù.
examples.english: The children hold hands while crossing the road.

examples.chinese: 他们见面时，总是会拉手(他們見面時，總是會拉手)。
examples.pinyin: Tāmen jiànmiàn shí, zǒng shì huì lāshǒu.
examples.english: When they meet, they always hold hands.
